current_run.description: This problem involves finding a secret binary string s of length N. The oracle computes f(x) = 1 if the majority of the bitwise products of adjacent bits in x are equal to the corresponding bits in s, and f(x) = 0 otherwise. Specifically, for an input x = x_{N-1}...x_1x_0, let p_i = x_i * x_{i-1} for i = 1 to N-1. The function returns 1 if the count of indices i where p_i == s_i is greater than (N-1)/2, and 0 otherwise. This is a non-linear function due to the bitwise products and the majority function.
